$description: First-class MOTION tokens. These promote taste-guardrails.md §3 (Pacing Rules) and §4.1 (signature easing curves) from prose into machine-checkable data, so the cinematic-doctor token-conformance check (Phase 8) and the Mode A/Mode B emitters (Phase 2) consume one source of truth. Easing curves are exact from taste-guardrails §4.1. GSAP equivalents are carried under $extensions for Mode B.

ease.$description: The four signature curves. Default CSS easings (ease/ease-in-out/linear) are BANNED (taste-guardrails §4.1) — always reference one of these by role.

reveal.$description: Dramatic deceleration — the 'reveal' feel. Hero entrances, title arrivals.

exit.$description: Clean acceleration — the 'handoff' feel. Chapter exits.

playful.$description: Overshoot — the 'playful' feel. Micro-interactions, hand-placed pops.

cut.$description: Heavy, deliberate — the 'chapter cut' feel. Whole-scene transitions.

duration.$description: Timed (non-scroll-scrubbed) animation durations. Scroll-driven motion is governed by motion.pacing, not these.

stagger.$description: Per-element stagger as a fraction of pin progress (taste-guardrails §3.6: 5-8%, max 5 elements before overlap).

pacing.$description: Scroll-pacing constants from taste-guardrails.md §3, expressed as numbers. Units noted per token. Mode A/B and the doctor read these as the canonical pin-range and rhythm budget.

pin-min-vh.$description: Minimum pin height in vh (§3.2). Below this a pin reads as a glitch.

pin-max-vh.$description: Maximum pin height in vh (§3.3). Beyond this users think the page froze; split with breathing room.

breathing-vh.$description: Minimum free-scroll between pinned chapters (§3.4) — the 'cut'.

title-reveal-start.$description: Title choreography occupies 30-40% of pin range (§3.5); start fraction.

title-reveal-end.$description: Upper bound of the title-reveal window; payoff lives in the final ~30%.

snap-dead-zone-vh.$description: Never snap within 10vh of a pin boundary (§3.7).

density-max.$description: Max simultaneous motion types per 50vh window (§3.8).

rhythm-sec-per-100vh.$description: Baseline scroll time: 1.2s per 100vh of content (§3.1).

depth.$description: Parallax scroll-rate multipliers (0 = fixed, 1 = scrolls with page). A reference ladder — taste-guardrails §4.3 forbids repeating the same multiplier across adjacent chapters, so themes/components vary these intentionally.

max-layers.$description: taste-guardrails §1.7: never more than 7 depth layers per chapter.
